title: Where the Cost Goes
concept: Embedding calls, LLM generation tokens, reranking calls, and vector DB storage as the major cost centers.

exercises.prompt: Welcome to Unit 476! Let's do a quick recap on RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation). What is the primary purpose of RAG in LLM apps?

options.text: Connecting the LLM to external databases for context
Changing the core weights of the model
Making the model generate words faster

exercises.explain: An LLM application uses external data sources (like databases or APIs) to provide accurate, up-to-date context directly to the language model.

cards.title: Where Does the Money Go?
cards.body: Building an LLM app is exciting, but the monthly API bill can quickly get scary! To keep costs low, we first need to pinpoint exactly where our money is being spent behind the scenes.

cards.title: The Four Cost Centers
cards.body: In LLM applications, costs are split into four main areas: vector DB storage (holding your data), embedding calls (translating search queries), reranking (sorting search results), and generation tokens (the words the LLM produces).

cards.title: The Bakery Bill
cards.body: Think of building an LLM app like running a bakery. Vector database storage is your monthly shop rent, embedding and reranking are the costs of sorting and selecting the best ingredients, and generation tokens are the actual flour and sugar you bake into the final cakes.

cards.title: A Single Query's Cost Lifecycle
cards.body: When a user searches a customer service bot, the app pays a fraction of a cent to embed the search, another fraction to search and store vectors, a small fee to rerank the top answers, and a larger fee for the LLM to write out the final response.

cards.title: The Hidden Iceberg of Cost
cards.body: Many developers think the final LLM text generation is the only cost. In reality, storing millions of high-dimensional vectors and constantly running search queries can easily become the most expensive part of your system.

cards.title: Monthly Rent vs. Pay-Per-Word
cards.body: Different steps in your application have different payment structures. Some charge by the word, while others charge by the gigabyte.

left.text: Subscription-like: Pays for hosting data over time.

right.text: Pay-as-you-go: Pays only for the words read and written.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary role of a reranking call in a search pipeline?

options.text: To translate input text into math vectors
To sort and select the most relevant search results before prompting the LLM
To store permanent document embeddings on a hard drive
To generate creative images for the user interface

exercises.explain: Reranking acts as a secondary, smart filter to sort retrieved documents by relevance before passing them to the expensive LLM.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Vector database storage is billed per individual API call, just like embedding generation.
exercises.explain: Vector database storage is typically billed on a monthly hosting model based on data size, while embeddings are billed per API call.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about LLM consumption.
exercises.sentence: The final text response created by the LLM is billed based on the number of generation tokens processed.
exercises.explain: Generation tokens are the primary unit of consumption when an LLM reads prompts and writes out final text.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following descriptions of vector database costs sounds like it was written by an AI?

options.text: Every time a user asks a question, your app has to pay to convert that question into numbers using an embedding model.
The vector database pipeline initiates cost accumulation through multi-dimensional index updates and retrieval operations.
Storing your documents in a vector database isn't free; you pay a recurring fee to keep those indices loaded in memory.

exercises.explain: Option B is AI-generated because it uses overly formal, formulaic language to describe the pipeline steps, whereas Options A and C focus on simple, practical explanations of cost.

exercises.prompt: Imagine your app has zero active users today. Which of these cost centers will still charge you a continuous, monthly fee just to keep your application's data alive?
exercises.explain: Vector storage fees are ongoing charges based on how much data you keep active (like monthly rent), while embedding, reranking, and generation costs are transaction-based, charging you only when users make requests.
